description_long: This is a modern, sleek fighter jet with a sharply pointed nose and a streamlined fuselage, predominantly colored in red, gray, and black. The wings are swept back with angular tips and feature red accents along the leading and trailing edges, giving it an aggressive, high-speed appearance. Twin vertical stabilizers rise from the rear, each adorned with a yellow insignia, and the tail section is robust with a pair of jet exhausts visible. The cockpit is forward-mounted, enclosed with a dark, tinted canopy, and appears to be designed for a single pilot. The undercarriage is not visible, suggesting retractable landing gear, and the jet is equipped with external hardpoints for armaments or fuel tanks. The overall design is reminiscent of advanced military aircraft, optimized for maneuverability and speed.
description: A modern, angular fighter jet with swept-back wings, twin vertical stabilizers, and a forward, enclosed cockpit, finished in red, gray, and black.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, forward cockpit, and wide, swept-back wings with visible jet intakes.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the angular wing design, twin vertical stabilizers, and external hardpoints under the wings.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, twin tail fins, and the streamlined, aerodynamic shape of the fuselage.
description_view_3: Right side view mirroring the left, with clear visibility of the tail section, wing structure, and external attachments.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, swept-back wings with angular tips and slight anhedral angle, no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted at the rear fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizers with a conventional horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear (not visible, implied by design).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a tinted canopy.
